key_insight: Finite element accuracy for mixed-boundary elliptic problems depends critically on quadrature precision: to preserve optimal convergence, volume and face integrals must be integrated accurately enough relative to polynomial order p.
engineering_relevance: For practical FEM implementations, this gives a clear rule for choosing quadrature rules so mesh refinement delivers the expected H1 and L2 error rates without hidden loss of accuracy.
method: Analyzed p-th order FEM for elliptic problems with mixed Dirichlet and Robin boundary conditions.
Derived minimum quadrature precision requirements for volume terms and boundary face terms.
Verified the theory with numerical experiments and sharpness examples for linear FEM.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimal H1 convergence with volume precision at least 2p-2 and face precision at least 2p-1, and optimal L2 convergence under the stated precision thresholds, confirmed by numerical experiments.
watch_item: Using under-integrated quadrature can break theoretical convergence rates, especially for low-order elements and boundary-face terms in mixed boundary settings.
paper_title: The effect of numerical integration in the FEM for elliptic problems with mixed boundary conditions
